phase.guide.1_0: Assessment. YOU (orchestrator) do this directly — read the task description, explore the codebase structure, and identify affected areas. Resume your plan-advisor teammate for guidance. Post research discussions (type='research') for questions that need investigation. Call workspace_update_progress when done.
phase.guide.1_1: Research. Deploy parallel researcher SUB-AGENTS (one per research discussion). Each researcher calls workspace_save_research with typed proofs AND discussion_id linking to the question it answers. Do NOT research yourself — delegate to sub-agents. After all researchers complete, review findings for gaps — post new research discussions if needed. When confident, advance with no_further_research_needed=True.
phase.guide.1_2: Research Proving. Deploy a research-prover SUB-AGENT (Opus). The prover calls workspace_prove_research for each entry — you do NOT call it yourself. Wait for the prover to finish. All entries must be confirmed to advance.
phase.guide.1_3:
Impact Analysis. YOU (orchestrator) analyze the research findings and determine:
1. What high-level areas are affected (user-facing flows, APIs, external integrations)
2. Any external dependencies or actions needed (DB migrations, infrastructure changes, team coordination)
3. Review and propose acceptance criteria via workspace_propose_criteria
Call workspace_update_progress with phase '1.3' summarizing affected areas and criteria decisions.
phase.guide.1_4: Preparation Review (USER GATE). Research summaries, impact analysis, and discussions are ready for user review. Do NOT proceed — wait for the user to approve or reject in the admin panel. If rejected, the workflow returns to Research (1.1) for further investigation.
phase.guide.2_0: Planning. Resume your plan-advisor TEAMMATE. Create the execution plan with sub-phases. Use workspace_set_scope to define the phase-keyed scope map and workspace_set_plan for the plan. Call workspace_update_progress when done.
phase.guide.2_1: Plan Review (USER GATE). The plan is awaiting user approval in the admin panel. Do NOT proceed — wait for the user to approve or reject.
phase.guide.4_0: Agentic Code Review. Deploy exactly 3 code-reviewer SUB-AGENTS in parallel, each reviewing from a different perspective (e.g. clean code & SOLID, architecture & data flow, edge cases & error handling). Do NOT brief them with implementation context — provide only the task description and branch. Reviewers call workspace_submit_review_issue for critical/major issues. Call workspace_update_progress('4.0') when all 3 reviewers complete.
phase.guide.4_1: Address Review Findings. Call workspace_get_review_issues to see all items. Deploy engineer SUB-AGENTS to fix legitimate issues — they call workspace_resolve_review_issue('fixed'). Mark false positives or out-of-scope items yourself. All review items must then be resolved by the user in the admin panel. Call workspace_update_progress('4') when done.
phase.guide.4_2: User Approval (USER GATE). All work complete. Awaiting user's final approval in admin panel. Do NOT proceed — wait for the user.
phase.guide.5: Done. Commit any remaining changes, then push and create MR/PR. Do this DIRECTLY — do NOT delegate git push or MR creation to a sub-agent.
phase.done.complete: Task complete! The workflow has finished successfully. Push and MR/PR creation are now available.
phase.guide.sub.0: Implementation. Deploy engineer SUB-AGENTS for production code, then test engineer SUB-AGENTS for tests (separate agents — never same agent for code + tests). Code edits are ON within the phase scope.
phase.guide.sub.1: Validation. Deploy validator SUB-AGENTS to review the implementation. Do NOT brief them with how the code was implemented — provide only the task description and phase scope. Results go to workspace/validation/3.{n}.json.
phase.guide.sub.2: Fixes. Deploy engineer SUB-AGENTS to address validation issues and/or user review comments (check workspace_get_comments). Code edits are ON within the phase scope. Advance when fixes are complete — this returns to Code Review for re-approval.
phase.guide.sub.3:
Code Review (USER GATE). Your changes are awaiting user review in the admin panel. Do NOT proceed — wait for the user to approve or reject.

If APPROVED → phase advances to Commit (3.N.4). Commit your changes.
If REJECTED → phase reverts to Fixes (3.N.2). Read user comments via workspace_get_comments, fix the issues, then call workspace_advance to return to Code Review. This cycle repeats until the user approves.
phase.guide.sub.4: Commit. Do this DIRECTLY (orchestrator) — do NOT delegate to a sub-agent. Stage and commit all changes. Provide commit_hash when calling workspace_advance. Call workspace_update_progress for phase '3.{n}'.
mcp.instructions: Workspace state management for orchestrator workflow.
mcp.tool.getState.desc:
Get compact workspace state overview. Returns core state (phase, scope, context, discussions) inline,
    with summaries and counts for large sections.

    For full details, use dedicated tools:
    - Plan: workspace_get_plan
    - Progress details: workspace_get_progress
    - Research: workspace_list_research / workspace_get_research
    - Comments: workspace_get_comments
    - Review issues: workspace_get_review_issues
    - Criteria: workspace_get_criteria

    Does NOT return gate_nonce (security: only available via admin panel UI).
mcp.tool.getState.detail.plan: workspace_get_plan — full plan with sub-phase tasks, scope, and system diagram
mcp.tool.getState.detail.progress: workspace_get_progress(phase?) — full progress entries with structured details
mcp.tool.getState.detail.research: workspace_get_research(ids=[...]) — full findings with typed proofs
mcp.tool.getState.detail.comments: workspace_get_comments(scope?, unresolved_only?) — review comments with file/line info
mcp.tool.getState.detail.reviewIssues: workspace_get_review_issues(status?) — review items with resolution status
mcp.tool.getState.detail.criteria: workspace_get_criteria(status?, type?) — acceptance criteria with details and validation
mcp.tool.advance.desc:
Request phase advancement. Provide commit_hash when at commit phases (3.N.4).

    At phase 1.1 (research → proving), you MUST set no_further_research_needed=True to confirm
    you have gathered all necessary information. If you're unsure, review your research findings
    against the research discussions — post new discussions and run more research if gaps exist.

    User gates (1.4, 2.1, 3.N.3, 4.2) require human approval via admin panel — advance returns 409.
    When the user REJECTS at a gate, phase reverts to the previous step (e.g. 3.N.3 → 3.N.2).
    Read user comments via workspace_get_comments, fix the issues, then call workspace_advance
    to return to the gate for re-review. Do NOT ask the user to approve in order to fix — fix first, then re-submit.
mcp.tool.setScope.desc:
Set workspace scope as a phase-keyed map. Allowed from phase 1 onwards.

    Setting scope automatically revokes approval — the user must review and re-approve
    the new scope in the admin panel before code edits are allowed.

    Format: {"3.1": {"must": ["src/models/"], "may": ["src/config/"]}, "3.2": {"must": [...], "may": [...]}}
    Each key is a sub-phase ID from the execution plan. 'must' directories MUST have changes for the phase to advance.
    'may' directories are permitted but not required.
mcp.tool.setPlan.desc:
Set the execution plan. Editable during and after planning (phase >= 2.0).

    The plan can be updated freely during planning. Once the user approves the plan
    in the admin panel, approval is revoked each time the plan is updated — the user
    must review and re-approve before the workflow can advance past planning.

    The previous plan is saved automatically — call workspace_restore_plan to revert
    if the new plan hasn't been approved yet.
mcp.tool.getPlan.desc:
Get the full execution plan including system diagram and all sub-phases with tasks.

    Returns the complete plan JSON: description, systemDiagram, and execution array with
    tasks per sub-phase. Use workspace_get_state to see the current scope map.
mcp.tool.restorePlan.desc:
Restore the previous plan version, swapping it with the current plan.

    Only works if current plan is NOT approved. If you need to revert an incorrectly
    set plan, call this before the user approves it.

    The previous plan's phase position is also restored — you'll return to wherever
    you were when that plan was active.
mcp.tool.postDiscussion.desc:
Raise an open discussion point — architectural decisions, research questions, TBDs.

    These are NOT chat messages. They are important topics that need research, discussion,
    or decision from either the agent or the human. Examples:
    - 'Should we use event-driven logging or centralized logger?'
    - 'Hibernate Envers vs custom audit implementation'
    - 'Async vs sync approach for trade operation logging'

    Discussions are visible in the admin panel where either side can add context and resolve them.
    Use parent_id to reply to an existing discussion (0 = new root discussion).
    type: 'general' (default) or 'research'. Research discussions represent questions
    that need investigation — they are tracked and must have linked research findings
    before the workflow can advance past the research phase.
mcp.tool.saveResearch.desc:
Save research findings. Called by researcher sub-agents after investigation.

    discussion_id: Optional — ID of the research discussion this answers. Link your research
    to the discussion that raised the question. Required for advancing past research phase
    (all unresolved research discussions must have linked findings).
mcp.tool.listResearch.desc:
List all research entries for the current workspace. Returns id, topic, findings count, and proven status.

    Does NOT return full findings content — use workspace_get_research to retrieve specific entries.
mcp.tool.getResearch.desc:
Get full research entries by IDs. Use for detailed review or proving.

    Returns complete findings with proofs for each requested ID.
mcp.tool.proveResearch.desc:
Mark a research entry as proven or rejected. Called by prover agent after verification.

    - id: research entry ID
    - proven: True if findings verified, False if rejected
    - notes: optional explanation of verification result
mcp.tool.getComments.desc: Get review comments, optionally filtered by scope. Returns list of comment objects (empty list if none).
mcp.tool.resolveComment.desc:
Mark a comment as resolved. Only works for non-review scoped comments — review items (scope='review') can only be resolved by the user in the admin panel. Use workspace_resolve_review_issue to set the resolution instead.

    - comment_id: the ID of the comment
    - Only resolves comments belonging to the current workspace
mcp.tool.submitReviewIssue.desc:
Submit a code review finding. Only critical and major issues are saved — minor/style issues are dropped.

    - file_path: path relative to workspace root (e.g. 'src/main/kotlin/Service.kt')
    - line_start: first line of the problematic code
    - line_end: last line of the problematic code
    - severity: 'critical' or 'major'
    - description: what the issue is and why it matters
mcp.tool.getReviewIssues.desc:
Get all review items for the current workspace.

    - status: filter by resolution ('open', 'fixed', 'false_positive', 'out_of_scope'). Empty = all.
    Returns list with id, file_path, lines, description, resolution, author, resolved.
mcp.tool.resolveReviewIssue.desc:
Set the resolution on a review item. Works on any review comment or finding (scope='review').

    - issue_id: the review item ID
    - resolution:
      - 'fixed': code was changed to address the issue
      - 'false_positive': issue is invalid, code is correct as-is
      - 'out_of_scope': legitimate issue but outside the allowed scope
mcp.tool.updateProgress.desc:
Update progress for a phase. Called by orchestrator after completing phase work.

    - phase: the phase key (e.g. "1.0", "1", "2", "3.1", "4")
    - summary: concise summary of what was done (1-3 sentences)
    - details: rich structured record of the phase work.
mcp.tool.getProgress.desc:
Get progress entries with full details. Optionally filter by phase key.

    - phase: specific phase key (e.g. "1.0", "2.0"). Empty = all phases.

    Returns dict of phase → {summary, details, created_at, updated_at}.
mcp.tool.proposeCriteria.desc:
Propose an acceptance criterion for the workspace. Called by the agent to suggest verifiable criteria.

    - type: one of 'unit_test', 'integration_test', 'bdd_scenario', 'custom'
    - description: human-readable description of what must pass
    - details_json: optional JSON string with type-specific details (test names, file paths, scenario steps)

    Proposed criteria are visible in the admin panel where the user can accept or reject them.
mcp.tool.getCriteria.desc:
Get acceptance criteria for the current workspace, optionally filtered.

    - status: filter by status ('proposed', 'accepted', 'rejected'). Empty = all.
    - type: filter by type ('unit_test', 'integration_test', 'bdd_scenario', 'custom'). Empty = all.

    Returns list of criteria with id, type, description, details, source, status, validated, validation_message.
mcp.tool.updateCriteria.desc:
Update an existing acceptance criterion's description and/or details. Use this to fill in
    file paths, test names, and refined descriptions for criteria created by the user.

    - criterion_id: ID of the criterion to update
    - description: updated description (optional, keeps existing if empty)
    - details_json: updated details JSON (optional, keeps existing if empty)
mcp.tool.postComment.desc:
Post a review comment on specific file lines. Used by code-reviewer agents.

    - file_path: path relative to workspace root
    - line_start/line_end: line range being commented on
    - text: the review comment
    - parent_id: reply to existing comment (0 = new root comment)
mcp.error.parentCommentNotFound: Parent comment {id} not found in this workspace
mcp.error.noWorkspace: No workspace found for current directory
mcp.error.scopePhase0: Scope cannot be set at phase 0. Start assessment first (advance to phase 1).
mcp.error.planPhase: Plan can only be set from planning phase onwards (phase >= 2)
mcp.error.planApproved: Current plan is approved. Cannot restore — follow the approved plan.
mcp.error.noFindings: At least one finding is required
mcp.error.commentNotFound: Comment {comment_id} not found in this workspace
mcp.error.invalidSeverity: Only 'critical' and 'major' severity issues should be submitted
mcp.error.lineStartBeyondFile: line_start {line_start} is beyond file length ({length} lines)
mcp.error.cannotValidateUnresolved: Cannot validate an unresolved issue. Resolve it first.
mcp.error.reviewScopeResolveBlocked: Review items can only be resolved by the user in the admin panel
mcp.error.invalidCriteriaType: Invalid type '{type}'. Must be one of: {valid_types}
mcp.error.cannotUpdateAcceptedCriteria: Cannot update accepted criteria. Ask the user to reject it first.
mcp.error.nothingToUpdate: Nothing to update. Provide description and/or details_json.
mcp.error.scopeNoteRevoked: Scope approval revoked. User must re-approve in admin panel.
mcp.error.planNoteRevoked: Plan and scope approval revoked. User must re-approve both in admin panel.
mcp.restorePlan.message: Previous plan restored. Phase set to {phase}
advance.error.noProgress: Call workspace_update_progress to record {phase} findings before {next}.
advance.error.noAssessmentProgress: Call workspace_update_progress to record assessment findings before research.
advance.error.noResearchDiscussion: Post at least one research discussion (type='research') via workspace_post_discussion before advancing. These define what questions need investigation.
advance.error.noFurtherResearch: You must confirm no further research is needed by passing no_further_research_needed=True. Review your research findings against the research discussions — do you have all the information needed to proceed to planning? If not, post new research discussions and run more research first.
advance.error.missingResearch: Unresolved research discussions without linked research findings. Either save research for each (with discussion_id), or resolve discussions that don't need research.
advance.error.noResearchEntries: No research entries found. Deploy researcher sub-agents to save findings via workspace_save_research.
advance.error.emptyFindings: Findings must be a non-empty array.
advance.error.codeProofMissingLineRange: Finding {index}: code proof missing line range.
advance.error.rejectedEntries: {count} research entries rejected. Re-deploy researchers to fix, then re-prove.
advance.error.unprovenEntries: {count} research entries not yet proven. Deploy prover sub-agent.
advance.error.noResearchCompletionProgre: Call workspace_update_progress to record research completion.
advance.error.noImpactAnalysisProgress: Call workspace_update_progress with phase '1.3' to record impact analysis before advancing.
advance.error.scopeAndPlanMustBeApproved: Scope and plan must both be approved before proceeding.
advance.error.noPlanExecution: Plan must have at least one execution item.
advance.error.planItemTasksMustBeArray: Item {i}: 'tasks' must be a non-empty array.
advance.error.planTaskFilesMustBeArray: Item {i}, task {ti}: 'files' must be an array.
advance.error.noCriteria: At least one acceptance criterion must be defined before advancing to plan review.
advance.error.noPlanningProgress: Call workspace_update_progress to record planning before approval.
advance.error.noAgenticReviewProgress: Call workspace_update_progress with phase '4.0' to record agentic review results before advancing.
advance.error.noFixAddressingProgress: Call workspace_update_progress to record fix addressing before completion.
advance.error.openReviewIssues: {count} review issue(s) still open. Resolve all issues with workspace_resolve_review_issue.
advance.error.unvalidatedIssues: {count} resolved issue(s) not yet validated. Deploy review-validator agent to verify all resolutions.
advance.error.validationRejected: Validation rejected for issue(s) {ids}. Fix and re-validate before advancing.
advance.error.issueStillPresent: Issue #{id} ({file_path}) marked as fixed but original code is still present. The code must actually be changed.
advance.error.noMustScopeChanges: No changes in must-scope entry '{pattern}'. Engineers must complete all required areas.
advance.error.commitAlreadyUsed: Commit {commit_hash} was already used in a previous sub-phase. Provide a new commit.
advance.error.noSubPhaseProgress: Call workspace_update_progress to record sub-phase completion.
advance.success.planValidated: Plan validated. {count} sub-phases. Awaiting user approval.
gate.error.pendingCriteria: {count} acceptance criteria are not yet accepted. All criteria must be accepted or deleted before approving the plan.
guard.review.unresolvedItems: {count} review item(s) still unresolved. All review items must be resolved by the user before advancing.
api.error.invalidStatus: Invalid status. Must be: pending, approved, rejected
api.error.invalidCriteriaType: Invalid type. Must be one of: {valid_types}
api.error.onlyCustomCriteriaManualValida: Only custom criteria can be manually validated
api.error.refsMustBeListOfStrings: refs must be a list of strings
api.error.sourceBranchNotFound: Source branch '{source}' not found locally or on remote
api.error.cannotCreateWorktreeCheckedOut: Cannot create worktree: branch '{branch}' is currently checked out. Use non-worktree mode or choose a different branch.
api.scope.planNotApproved: Plan not approved. User must approve the plan before code changes.
api.scope.scopeNotApproved: Scope not approved. User must approve the scope before code changes.
api.scope.fileOutsideApprovedScope: File '{file_path}' is outside the approved scope.
improvements.resolvePrompt: Add a note about what was done (optional):
verification.noProfiles: No verification profiles assigned. Select one below to get started.
